CheckTitle: AI agent must not have API keys or tokens committed to version control
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Credentials**. Verifies that no API keys, access tokens, or secrets have been committed to the version control history of the AI agent's repositories.
Risk: Secrets committed to version control are permanent: even after deletion they remain in the git history and can be recovered. Automated scanners (GitGuardian, TruffleHog) continuously index both public and private repositories for such secrets.

Code.Other:
1. Run Trufflehog or Gitleaks against the full repository history to identify exposed secrets.
2. Revoke and rotate every discovered credential immediately.
3. Use git-filter-repo to remove secrets from history, then force-push.
4. Add .gitignore rules for secrets files and enable pre-commit secret scanning hooks.
5. Configure your VCS provider's built-in secret scanning alerts.

Recommendation.Text: Enable secret scanning on all repositories. Rotate any discovered credentials immediately. Add pre-commit hooks and CI gates to prevent future secret commits.

Notes: ASPM-029 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
